devanagari: नारद उवाच । इत्थं पुरञ्जनं सध्र्यग् वशमानीय विभ्रमैः । पुरञ्जनी महाराज रेमे रमयती पतिम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvāca itthaṃ purañjanaṃ sadhryag vaśamānīya vibhramaiḥ purañjanī mahārāja reme ramayatī patim

translation:
Nārada said:
In this way, bringing Purañjana under her complete control by her womanly charms, Purañjanī (the queen of Purañjana) enjoyed her life, giving delight to her husband, Oh great king.

devanagari: स राजा महिषीं राजन् सुस्नातां रुचिराननाम् । कृतस्वस्त्ययनां तृप्तां अभ्यनन्ददुपागताम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sa rājā mahiṣīṃ rājan susnātāṃ rucirānanām kṛtasvastyayanāṃ tṛptāṃ abhyanandadupāgatām

translation: When the queen who took her full bath and beautified herself with auspicious decorations, approached the king fully satisfied and with bright countenance, he hailed her with joy, Oh king (Prācīnabarhis).

devanagari: तयोपगूढः परिरब्धकन्धरो । रहोऽनुमन्त्रैरपकृष्टचेतनः । न कालरंहो बुबुधे दुरत्ययं । दिवा निशेति प्रमदापरिग्रहः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tayopagūḍhaḥ parirabdhakandharo raho na kālaraṃho bubudhe duratyayaṃ divā niśeti pramadāparigrahaḥ

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Embraced by her, he threw his arms about her neck. He lost his reasoning capacity by the amorous sweet words of the queen in privacy. As he was thus possessed by the young lady day and night, he did not grasp the inexorably rapid course of time.

devanagari: शयान उन्नद्धमदो महामना । महार्हतल्पे महिषीभुजोपधिः । तामेव वीरो मनुते परं यतः । तमोऽभिभूतो न निजं परं च यत् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śayāna unnaddhamado mahāmanā mahārhatalpe mahiṣībhujopadhiḥ tāmeva vīro manute paraṃ yataḥ tamo

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The magnanimous prince, being overwhelmed with lustful passion, lay on a very costly couch with the queen’s arm as a pillow. He regarded her as the supreme objective in life (puruṣārtha) and he did not think of his real transcendental self[1] (or Brahman) as he was over-powered with Tamas (ignorance).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Or alternatively he did not know what was his and what was another’s.

devanagari: तयैवं रममाणस्य कामकश्मलचेतसः । क्षणार्धमिव राजेन्द्र व्यतिक्रान्तं नवं वयः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tayaivaṃ ramamāṇasya kāmakaśmalacetasaḥ kṣaṇārdhamiva rājendra vyatikrāntaṃ navaṃ vayaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Oh king of kings! While he was enjoying pleasures thus with his mind clouded with passion, his youth passed away as if it were half a minute.

devanagari: तस्यां अजनयत् पुत्रान् पुरञ्जन्यां पुरञ्जनः । शतान्येकादश विराड् आयुषोऽर्धमथात्यगात् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ ajanayat putrān purañjanyāṃ purañjanaḥ śatānyekādaśa virāḍ āyuṣo
translation: [2] The emperor Purañjana begot through (empress) Purañjanī eleven hundred sons. But he lost half of his life then.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Here ‘eleven’ signifies the ten sense organs and the mind. ‘Hundreds’ means innumerable activities of senses.
Daughters are the mental activities.

devanagari: दुहित्‍ईर्दशोत्तरशतं पितृमातृयशस्करीः । शीलौदार्यगुणोपेताः पौरञ्जन्यः प्रजापते ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: duhit‍īrdaśottaraśataṃ pitṛmātṛyaśaskarīḥ śīlaudāryaguṇopetāḥ paurañjanyaḥ prajāpate
translation: Oh Lord of creatures! He (also) begot eleven hundred and ten daughters called Paurañjanīs. They brought glory to their parents and were endowed with qualities like good character and generosity.

devanagari: स पञ्चालपतिः पुत्रान् पितृवंशविवर्धनान् । दारैः संयोजयामास दुहितॄ सदृशैर्वरैः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa pañcālapatiḥ putrān pitṛvaṃśavivardhanān dāraiḥ saṃyojayāmāsa duhitṝ sadṛśairvaraiḥ
translation: [3] That king of Pañcālas (Purañjana) got his sons, (all) capable of continuing the race of their forefathers, married to suitable brides and his daughters to proper bridegrooms.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Jīva, the Lord of five senses, married his sons i.e. sense-activities to suitable brides, i.e. thoughts about what is beneficial or otherwise. His daughters i.e. mental activities were associated with virtues like modesty or proper objectives of pleasure.

devanagari: पुत्राणां चाभवन् पुत्रा एकैकस्य शतं शतम् । यैर्वै पौरञ्जनो वंशः पञ्चालेषु समेधितः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: putrāṇāṃ cābhavan putrā ekaikasya śataṃ śatam yairvai paurañjano vaṃśaḥ pañcāleṣu samedhitaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: And his son had each a hundred sons who have verily propagated the race of Purañjana in the Pañcāla country.

devanagari: तेषु तद् रिक्थहारेषु गृहकोशानुजीविषु । निरूढेन ममत्वेन विषयेष्वन्वबध्यत ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: teṣu tad rikthahāreṣu gṛhakośānujīviṣu nirūḍhena mamatvena viṣayeṣvanvabadhyata
translation: By the formation of a strong attachment (lit. sense of mineness) to (sons and grandsons), the shareholders in his property, house treasures and dependants (e.g. ministers, servants), he became firmly attached to the sense-objects.

devanagari: ईजे च क्रतुभिर्घोरैः दीक्षितः पशुमारकैः । देवान् पितॄन् भूतपतीन् नानाकामो यथा भवान् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: īje ca kratubhirghoraiḥ dīkṣitaḥ paśumārakaiḥ devān pitṝn bhūtapatīn nānākāmo yathā bhavān

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Being prompted by various desires he became consecrated for sacrifices and worshipped gods, manes (pitṛs), Lord of the goblins with sacrifices terrible on account of slaughter of beasts, as your honour has done.

devanagari: युक्तेष्वेवं प्रमत्तस्य कुटुम्बासक्तचेतसः । आससाद स वै कालो योऽप्रियः प्रिययोषिताम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yukteṣvevaṃ pramattasya kuṭumbāsaktacetasaḥ āsasāda sa vai kālo yo

translation: In this way, he remained remiss about his duties (spiritually) beneficial to him, and had his mind strongly attached to his family. Verily there came the period of life (old age) which is repellent to persons addicted to women.

devanagari: चण्डवेग इति ख्यातो गन्धर्वाधिपतिर्नृप । गन्धर्वास्तस्य बलिनः षष्ट्युत्तरशतत्रयम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: caṇḍavega iti khyāto gandharvādhipatirnṛpa gandharvāstasya balinaḥ ṣaṣṭyuttaraśatatrayam

translation: Oh King Prācīnabarhis! There was a King of Gandharvas, known as Caṇḍavega (signifying the complete year). The powerful king had a force of three hundred sixty Gandharvas (Gandharvas standing for days).

devanagari: गन्धर्व्यस्तादृशीरस्य मैथुन्यश्च सितासिताः । परिवृत्त्या विलुम्पन्ति सर्वकामविनिर्मिताम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: gandharvyastādṛśīrasya maithunyaśca sitāsitāḥ parivṛttyā vilumpanti sarvakāmavinirmitām

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ lup class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ lup class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ lup class 6 verb]

translation: Paired to (i.e. wedded to) them were similar (equal number of) Gandharva women (i.e. nights) some dark, some fair or white (according to the fortnights). By their rotational raids, they plundered the city furnished with all desired objects, since its creation.

devanagari: ते चण्डवेगानुचराः पुरञ्जनपुरं यदा । हर्तुमारेभिरे तत्र प्रत्यषेधत् प्रजागरः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: te caṇḍavegānucarāḥ purañjanapuraṃ yadā hartumārebhire tatra pratyaṣedhat prajāgaraḥ
translation: When those attendants of Caṇḍavega began to plunder the city of Purañjana, Prajāgara, the serpent-guard resisted them there.

devanagari: स सप्तभिः शतैरेको विंशत्या च शतं समाः । पुरञ्जनपुराध्यक्षो गन्धर्वैर्युयुधे बली ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa saptabhiḥ śataireko viṃśatyā ca śataṃ samāḥ purañjanapurādhyakṣo gandharvairyuyudhe balī

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: That powerful presiding deity of the city of Purañjana, single-handedly fought with seven hundred and twenty Gandharvas (male and female) for one hundred years (which is the duration of man’s life).

devanagari: क्षीयमाणे स्वसंबन्धे एकस्मिन् बहुभिर्युधा । चिन्तां परां जगामार्तः सराष्ट्रपुरबान्धवः ॥ १७ ॥

translation: When his relative—the guardian-serpent—began to lose strength by his single-handed fight with many, Purañjana, along with kingdom, capital and relations felt very much distressed with great anxiety.

devanagari: स एव पुर्यां मधुभुक् पञ्चालेषु स्वपार्षदैः । उपनीतं बलिं गृह्णन् स्त्रीजितो नाविदद्‍भयम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva puryāṃ madhubhuk pañcāleṣu svapārṣadaiḥ upanītaṃ baliṃ gṛhṇan strījito nāvidad‍bhayam

translation: In the capital city of Pañcāla he indulged in drinking and was under the thumb of women, while he used to receive taxes collected by his retainers, he was not aware of the danger.

devanagari: कालस्य दुहिता काचित्त्रिलोकीं वरमिच्छती । पर्यटन्ती न बर्हिष्मन् प्रत्यनन्दत कश्चन ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kālasya duhitā kācittrilokīṃ varamicchatī paryaṭantī na barhiṣman pratyanandata kaścana

translation: Oh king Barhiṣad (Prācīnabarhis)! There was a certain daughter of Kāla[4] (the Time-spirit) who, being desirous of having a husband, toured over the three worlds. But nobody welcomed her.

devanagari: दौर्भाग्येनात्मनो लोके विश्रुता दुर्भगेति सा । या तुष्टा राजर्षये तु वृतादात्पूरवे वरम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: daurbhāgyenātmano loke viśrutā durbhageti sā yā tuṣṭā rājarṣaye tu vṛtādātpūrave varam

translation: Due to her misfortune she was well-known over the world as Durbhagā (the unfortunate). She was (once) sought after by the royal-sage, Puru, to whom she gave a boon.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
This refers to Puru’s acceptance of the old-age of his father Yayāti to himself in exchange of his youth. Yayāti returned his youth to his son and made him the sovereign king after him.—P.I. 2,374.

devanagari: कदाचिद् अटमाना सा ब्रह्मलोकान् महीं गतम् । वव्रे बृहद्व्रतं मां तु जानती काममोहिता ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: kadācid aṭamānā sā brahmalokān mahīṃ gatam vavre bṛhadvrataṃ māṃ tu jānatī kāmamohitā

translation: One day while she was wandering, she met me when I descended from Brahmā’s region (satyaloka) to the earth. Being infatuated with passion, she sought me even though she knew that I had taken a vow of lifelong celibacy.

devanagari: मयि संरभ्य विपुलं अदात् शापं सुदुःसहम् । स्थातुमर्हसि नैकत्र मद् याच्ञाविमुखो मुने ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: mayi saṃrabhya vipulaṃ adāt śāpaṃ suduḥsaham sthātumarhasi naikatra mad yācñāvimukho mune

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Getting enraged (at my refusal), she gave me an extremely unbearable curse; ‘Oh Sage, as you have set your face against my importunity, you will never stay at one place.’

devanagari: ततो विहतसङ्कल्पा कन्यका यवनेश्वरम् । मयोपदिष्टमासाद्य वव्रे नाम्ना भयं पतिम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tato vihatasaṅkalpā kanyakā yavaneśvaram mayopadiṣṭamāsādya vavre nāmnā bhayaṃ patim

translation: The girl whose desire was so frustrated then followed my advice and approached the king of Yavanas,[6] by name Bhaya (Fear or Death), and courted him as a husband.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Yavanas signify physical pains and mental anguish.

devanagari: ऋषभं यवनानां त्वां वृणे वीरेप्सितं पतिम् । सङ्कल्पस्त्वयि भूतानां कृतः किल न रिष्यति ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣabhaṃ yavanānāṃ tvāṃ vṛṇe vīrepsitaṃ patim saṅkalpastvayi bhūtānāṃ kṛtaḥ kila na riṣyati

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ āp ], [accusative single from √ āp ]
[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: “Oh brave warrior! I woo you, the leader of the Yavanas, as my coveted husband. The hope entertained by the creatures in you is never falsified.[7]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
VC. takes bhūta as ‘the devotee of god’ and interprets:
‘The purpose of the devotees of the Lord is never frustrated’. Nārada intended that the ‘Fear’ (Bhaya) should grew old and weak by the marriage of Kālakanyā with him. His purpose should be achieved by my marrying you.
(ii) As Bhaya means Kāla (Death or Time) Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa adopts the second meaning ‘Time’ and explains: “A plan or determination made at the opportune (auspicious) time always bears fruit. I court you as you are the giver of fruit (success in undertaking) to all.”

devanagari: द्वौ इमौ अनुशोचन्ति बालौ असदवग्रहौ । यल्लोकशास्त्रोपनतं न राति न तदिच्छति ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: dvau imau anuśocanti bālau asadavagrahau yallokaśāstropanataṃ na rāti na tadicchati

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śuc class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śuc class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ śuc class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ trup class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ trup class 1 verb]

translation: Good people feel regret for those two (types of) foolish and perverse natured people who neither give nor accept what is offered without any request and which is worth giving or receiving according to custom or Vedic precept.

devanagari: अथो भजस्व मां भद्र भजन्तीं मे दयां कुरु । एतावान् पौरुषो धर्मो यदार्तान् अनुकम्पते ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: atho bhajasva māṃ bhadra bhajantīṃ me dayāṃ kuru etāvān pauruṣo dharmo yadārtān anukampate
translation: Therefore, Oh good Sir, accept me who am offering myself to you. Please be gracious to me. It is the bounden duty of man to be compassionate with the afflicted.”

devanagari: कालकन्योदितवचो निशम्य यवनेश्वरः । चिकीर्षुर्देवगुह्यं स सस्मितं तामभाषत ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: kālakanyoditavaco niśamya yavaneśvaraḥ cikīrṣurdevaguhyaṃ sa sasmitaṃ tāmabhāṣata
translation: Having heard the speech uttered by Kāla-kanyā (the daughter of Kāla), the lord of Yavanas, who desired to carry out the secret work of gods (viz., to bring about the death of living beings)[8] spoke to her with a smile.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Death is kept secret by gods, lest people should renunciate the world.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: मया निरूपितस्तुभ्यं पतिरात्मसमाधिना । नाभिनन्दति लोकोऽयं त्वां अभद्रां असम्मताम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: mayā nirūpitastubhyaṃ patirātmasamādhinā nābhinandati loko
translation: “With the help of my Yogic vision born of my power of meditation, I have foreseen and selected a husband for you (even if) this world does not welcome you as you are both disagreeable (undesirable) and inauspicious.

devanagari: त्वं अव्यक्तगतिर्भुङ्क्ष्व लोकं कर्मविनिर्मितम् । या हि मे पृतनायुक्ता प्रजानाशं प्रणेष्यसि ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ avyaktagatirbhuṅkṣva lokaṃ karmavinirmitam yā hi me pṛtanāyuktā prajānāśaṃ praṇeṣyasi
translation: Adopting unmanifest and imperceptible way of movement, you enjoy (each and every being in) the world created by karma. You go in company of my army (of diseases and anxieties) and you will bring about the destruction of all living beings.

devanagari: प्रज्वारोऽयं मम भ्राता त्वं च मे भगिनी भव । चराम्युभाभ्यां लोकेऽस्मिन् अव्यक्तो भीमसैनिकः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: prajvāro carāmyubhābhyāṃ loke
translation:
This Prajvāra (Fatal fever created by Viṣṇu) is my brother. You be my sister. Accompanied by both of you and my formidable soldiers (physical and mental ailments) I shall range this world imperceptibly.”
[back to top]
